seoTitle: How to Print a Reconciliation Report in QuickBooks
metaDescription: Print a reconciliation report in QuickBooks to document matched transactions for audits, budgeting, or record keeping.
intro: A printed reconciliation report gives you a physical or shareable record confirming your books match your bank statement, which is useful during audits or budget reviews. This guide covers navigating to the report and printing it in QuickBooks. It's aimed at small business owners and finance professionals who need documented proof of reconciliation.

steps.heading: Navigate the QuickBooks dashboard
steps.body: Find the reports section from your main QuickBooks dashboard.

steps.heading: Access reconciliation reports
steps.body: Locate the specific reconciliation report tied to the account and period you need.

steps.heading: Print the report
steps.body: Use the print option to generate a physical or PDF copy of the reconciliation.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot common issues
steps.body: If the report doesn't print correctly, check your printer settings or export as PDF instead.

faq.question: Can I save a reconciliation report as a PDF instead of printing it?
faq.answer: Yes, most reports offer a PDF export option alongside the print function.

faq.question: How far back can I print a reconciliation report?
faq.answer: This depends on your account's transaction history, but past reconciliations are generally accessible for review.

faq.question: Why would I need a printed reconciliation report?
faq.answer: It's often requested during audits or when sharing financial documentation with stakeholders who prefer paper records.
